Class WhileLoopTransformation

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.visitor.JavaASTWalker
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.visitor.JavaASTVisitor
de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.metaconstruct.WhileLoopTransformation
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visitor
Direct Known Subclasses:
ForToWhileTransformation, WhileInvariantTransformation
public class WhileLoopTransformation extends JavaASTVisitor
Walks through a java AST in depth-left-fist-order. This walker is used to transform a loop (not only while loops) according to the rules of the dynamic logic.

    performActionOnEnhancedFor(EnhancedFor x)
    perform the loop transformation on an enhanced for loop (Java5) If the enhanced for loop is the toplevel loop nothing happens - return the loop itself, as enhanced for loops cannot be unwound, a log message is issued.

    void
    performActionOnWhile(While x)
    Performs the unwinding of the loop Warning: The unwinding does not comply with the rule in the KeY book up to 100% The difference is revealed by the following example: Label1:while(c){b} According to the KeY book the transformation should be if(c) l':{l'':{p#} Label1:while(c){b}} This implementation creates however.

    replaceJumpStatement(LabelJumpStatement x)

    • replacement

      protected ProgramElement replacement
      the replacement element

    • breakOuterLabel

      protected Break breakOuterLabel
      break outerlabel

    • breakInnerLabel

      protected Break breakInnerLabel
      break innerlabel

    • labelList

      protected final ExtList labelList

    • stack

      protected final Deque<ExtList> stack

    • replaceBreakWithNoLabel

      protected int replaceBreakWithNoLabel
      if there is a loop inside the loop the breaks of these inner loops have not to be replaced. The replaceBreakWithNoLabel counts the depth of the loop cascades. Replacements are only performed if the value of the variable is zero.

    • runMode

      protected final int runMode
      there are two modes the visitor can be run. The check and transformation mode. In the check mode it is only looked if there are unlabeled break and continues that needs to be replaced, the transformation mode performs the unwinding of the loop with all necessary replacements

    • needOuterLabel

      protected boolean needOuterLabel
      indicates if an unlabled break has been found and an outer label is needed

    • needInnerLabel

      protected boolean needInnerLabel
      Indicates if an unlabled continue has been found or if a labelled continue with a label outside of the loop currently transformed has been found. Then an inner label is needed.

    • newLabels

      protected int newLabels
      counts the number of labelled continues with an label outside the while loop that is transformed

    • instantiations

      protected SVInstantiations instantiations
      if run in check mode there are normally schemavaribles, so we need the instantiations of them

    • result

      protected ProgramElement result
      the result of the transformation

    • labelStack

      protected final ArrayDeque<Label> labelStack

    • methodStack

      protected final ArrayDeque<MethodFrame> methodStack

    • WhileLoopTransformation

      public WhileLoopTransformation(ProgramElement root, ProgramElementName outerLabel, ProgramElementName innerLabel, Services services)
      creates the WhileLoopTransformation for the transformation mode
      Parameters:
      root - the ProgramElement where to begin
      outerLabel - the ProgramElementName of the outer label
      innerLabel - the ProgramElementName of the inner label
      services - services instance

      Parameters:
      root - the ProgramElement where to begin
      inst - the SVInstantiations if available
      services - services instance

    • performActionOnEnhancedFor

      public void performActionOnEnhancedFor(EnhancedFor x)
      perform the loop transformation on an enhanced for loop (Java5) If the enhanced for loop is the toplevel loop nothing happens - return the loop itself, as enhanced for loops cannot be unwound, a log message is issued. If it is a loop deeper in the AST a new object is created if needed or the original loop returned.
      Specified by:
      performActionOnEnhancedFor in interface Visitor
      Overrides:
      performActionOnEnhancedFor in class JavaASTVisitor
      Parameters:
      x - EnhancedFor loop statement

    • performActionOnWhile

      public void performActionOnWhile(While x)
      Performs the unwinding of the loop Warning: The unwinding does not comply with the rule in the KeY book up to 100% The difference is revealed by the following example: Label1:while(c){b} According to the KeY book the transformation should be if(c) l':{l'':{p#} Label1:while(c){b}} This implementation creates however. Label1:if(c) l':{l'':{p#} while(c){b}} Check if this is ok when labeled continue statements are involved.
      Specified by:
      performActionOnWhile in interface Visitor
      Overrides:
      performActionOnWhile in class JavaASTVisitor
      Parameters:
      x - the while statement
